'''Polus''' was an [[Water/Legends|ice]] [[Planet/Legends|world]] [[Orbit/Legends|orbiting]] the [[Binary star/Legends|binary star]] [[Avindia]] of the [[Avindia system]] in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]. A [[Temperature/Legends|frigid]], [[Mountain/Legends|mountainous]] planet, it had a massive [[Frozen Polus Ocean|frozen ocean]] and blizzards as well as ice storms that would frequently scour the planet's surface. Its average [[Standard day/Legends|day]] temperature was 50 degrees below freezing. Polus was the [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of the [[Pyn'gani]], a [[near-Human]] [[Species/Legends|species]].

==Description==
[[File:Polus-ground.jpg|thumb|left|200px|A Pyn'gani village situated around a shield generator]]
Polus was situated in the [[Avindia system]]<ref name="EAWGG" /> of the [[Oricho sector/Legends|Oricho sector]]<ref name="TEAOC" /> in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]] and had a diameter of 9,562 [[Kilometer/Legends|kilometers]].<ref name="EAWGG" /> The [[Planet/Legends|planet]] was located between the [[Braxant Run/Legends|Braxant Run]] and [[Celanon Spur/Legends|Celanon Spur]]<ref name="TEA" /> and orbited the [[Binary star/Legends|binary star]] [[Avindia]].<ref name="EAWGG" /> Polus was an arctic, mostly mountainous planet with one massive [[Frozen Polus Ocean|frozen ocean]]. The average daytime temperature was 50 below freezing with frequent blizzards and [[Water/Legends|ice]] storms and a brief but seasonal warming period when the planet received light from both of its [[Star/Legends|suns]]. Polus was also orbited by a [[Moon/Legends|moon]] and inhabited by [[Dral'k ice worm]]s as well as various other aquatic life forms.<ref name="EAW">''[[Star Wars: Empire at War]]''</ref><ref name="EAWGG" />

==History==
Polus was home to the [[Pyn'gani]], a [[near-Human]] [[Species/Legends|species]]. The Pyn'gani played a major role in creating [[Carbonite/Legends|carbon]] [[Carbon-freezing/Legends|freezing]], and [[Mining/Legends|mining]] produced a large proportion of income for Polus.<ref name="EAW" /><ref name="EAWGG" /> In [[995 BBY]], the Pyn'gani [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Anno Wen-Chii]] and his [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] [[Nalia Adollu]] traveled to the planet to study how life evolved in [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]].<ref name="Darth Bane: Rule of Two">''[[Darth Bane: Rule of Two]]''</ref><ref name="The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia">''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''</ref>

{{Gamemechanics}}
In [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]], Polus was a head-quarters of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]], equipped with a planetary [[Ion cannon/Legends|ion cannon]] and was attacked and captured by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]].<ref name="EAW" /><ref name="EAWGG" /> Around [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], the [[Zann Consortium/Legends|Zann Consortium]] raided the planet, [[Slavery/Legends|enslaving]] the native Pyn'gani.<ref name="EaW: FoC">''[[Star Wars: Empire at War: Forces of Corruption]]''</ref>
{{Endgame}}

==Inhabitants==
{{Main|Pyn'gani}}
The Pyn'gani were a near-Human species native to Polus. To protect them from the extreme cold of their [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]], their [[City/Legends|cities]] were shielded by [[heat generator]]s. The Pyn'gani played a major role in creating carbon freezing, and they received substantial income through mining.<ref name="EAW" /><ref name="EAWGG" /> The Planet had a population of 600,000 around the time of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], with the main spoken language was [[Pyn'gani language|that]] of the Pyn'gani itself.<ref name="EAWGG" /> The Pyn'gani feared foreigners, but were interested in new technology.<ref name="EaW: FoC" />

==Behind the scenes==
Polus first appeared in the game ''[[Star Wars: Empire at War]]'' and in the expansion pack ''[[Star Wars: Empire at War: Forces of Corruption]]''. The planet was mentioned in [[Star Wars: Empire at War: Prima Official Game Guide|the]] [[Star Wars: Empire at War: Forces of Corruption Expansion: Prima Official Game Guide|guides]] to the games, and received an entry in ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' as well as ''[[The Essential Atlas]]''.
